pkg/clock.purpose: Process-global test clock that replaces time.Now() calls with a controllable time source. All production code must call clock.Now() instead of time.Now() so tests can freeze or shift time deterministically.

patterns.name: clock.Now() everywhere
patterns.description: Production code must call clock.Now() not time.Now(). The package uses package-level atomics so no injection is needed.

patterns.name: FreezeTime/UnFreeze in tests
patterns.description: Tests freeze the clock with clock.FreezeTime(t) and unfreeze with defer clock.UnFreeze() or defer clock.ResetTime() to restore drift-based mode.

patterns.name: Monotonic clock stripped
patterns.description: clock.Now() always calls .Round(0) to strip the monotonic reading, preventing subtle comparison bugs when storing/comparing times.

key_file_guides.role: Entire package implementation: atomic drift int64 for SetTime, atomic frozen int32 + atomic.Value for FreezeTime.
key_file_guides.watch_for: SetTime computes drift as time.Since(t).Nanoseconds() — drift is the offset to subtract from wall time, not the absolute time.

anti_patterns: Calling time.Now() directly in production code — always use clock.Now()
Forgetting defer clock.ResetTime() or defer clock.UnFreeze() in tests, which leaks frozen state across test cases
Adding a context parameter or interface indirection — the global var design is intentional for zero-overhead usage

decisions.decision: Package-level atomics instead of an injected Clock interface
decisions.rationale: Zero call-site changes needed across the codebase; atomic ops are lock-free and safe for concurrent test use.

pkg/contextx.purpose: Context-keyed key-value store (via peterbourgon/ctxdata) and a slog.Handler adapter that auto-attaches those stored attributes to every log record emitted within a request context.

patterns.name: WithAttr / WithAttrs to annotate context
patterns.description: Call contextx.WithAttr(ctx, key, value) or contextx.WithAttrs(ctx, map) to attach structured data to the context. A ctxdata bag is lazily created if absent.

patterns.name: NewLogHandler wraps existing slog.Handler
patterns.description: Wrap any slog.Handler with contextx.NewLogHandler to automatically add all context attributes to every log record. Install at logger construction time.

key_file_guides.role: WithAttr and WithAttrs write to ctxdata; both silently ignore Set errors.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ctxdata.From returns nil if no bag exists — always handle the nil case by calling ctxdata.New before Set.

key_file_guides.role: Handler wraps slog.Handler; Handle reads ctxdata.GetAllMap and appends all entries as slog.Any attributes.
key_file_guides.watch_for: WithAttrs and WithGroup delegate to the inner handler and re-wrap — do not forget to preserve the Handler struct.

anti_patterns: Using context.WithValue directly for structured log fields instead of contextx.WithAttr
Installing contextx.NewLogHandler more than once in the handler chain (double-attaches the same attributes)

decisions.decision: peterbourgon/ctxdata as the context bag instead of custom context keys
decisions.rationale: Provides a typed map API accessible from any layer without defining package-private key types everywhere.

pkg/convert.purpose: Nil-safe generic pointer and type conversion helpers used throughout the codebase to avoid repetitive nil-guard boilerplate when mapping between domain types and API/Ent types.

patterns.name: ToPointer for value-to-pointer
patterns.description: Use convert.ToPointer[T](value) instead of taking the address of a local variable directly.

patterns.name: MapToPointer / SliceToPointer return nil on empty
patterns.description: Both functions return nil (not a pointer to empty collection) when the collection is empty. Use when an absent collection must differ from an empty one in JSON serialization.

patterns.name: SafeDeRef for nil-safe pointer transformation
patterns.description: SafeDeRef applies a mapping function to a pointer only if non-nil, otherwise returns nil. Replaces repetitive if ptr != nil checks in converters.

patterns.name: ToStringLike for type-aliased string conversions
patterns.description: Converts *Source to *Dest where both are ~string types, avoiding unsafe casts.

key_file_guides.role: All pointer/type helpers including DerefHeaderPtr for *map and *slice dereferencing.
key_file_guides.watch_for: lo.EmptyableToPtr is explicitly avoided for maps/slices — use MapToPointer/SliceToPointer instead.

key_file_guides.role: TimePtrIn converts *time.Time to a given location, nil-safe.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Returns a new pointer; does not mutate in place.

anti_patterns: Using lo.EmptyableToPtr on maps or slices — it does not treat empty collections as nil
Adding domain-specific conversion logic here — this package is for generic pointer/type mechanics only

pkg/currencyx.purpose: Thin ISO 4217 currency wrapper built on invopop/gobl/currency that adds a Calculator type for currency-precision decimal rounding, used across billing and subscription for monetary arithmetic.

patterns.name: Validate before Calculator
patterns.description: Always call Code.Validate() or obtain a Calculator via Code.Calculator() which validates internally. Never use a Code without validation.
patterns.example: calc, err := currencyx.Code("USD").Calculator(); if err != nil { return err }

patterns.name: Calculator.RoundToPrecision for monetary amounts
patterns.description: Round alpacadecimal.Decimal to the correct subunit count for the currency using Calculator.RoundToPrecision. Never hardcode decimal places.

key_file_guides.role: Code type (type alias of currency.Code), Validate, Calculator constructor, RoundToPrecision, IsRoundedToPrecision.
key_file_guides.watch_for: RoundToPrecision uses Def.Subunits (not smallest denomination) — fine for online payments but not for physical cash.

anti_patterns: Rounding monetary amounts with a fixed precision constant instead of Calculator.RoundToPrecision
Using currency.Code (gobl) directly instead of currencyx.Code — the wrapper adds Validate and Calculator

decisions.decision: Wraps gobl/currency rather than implementing ISO 4217 from scratch
decisions.rationale: GOBL is already a transitive dependency for invoicing; reusing its currency definitions avoids a duplicate definition table.

pkg/datetime.purpose: Billing-safe datetime and ISO 8601 duration library. DateTime wraps time.Time with RFC 9557 timezone-annotated formatting/parsing; ISODuration wraps rickb777/period with no-overflow month/year arithmetic and DivisibleBy for subscription cadence validation.

patterns.name: Parse for all datetime inputs
patterns.description: Use datetime.Parse(s) to accept RFC3339, ISO8601, and RFC9557 (timezone-annotated) strings. Never use time.Parse directly for user-supplied timestamps.

patterns.name: ISODurationString.Parse() for duration inputs
patterns.description: Accept durations as ISODurationString and call .Parse() to obtain ISODuration. Use ParsePtrOrNil for optional fields.

patterns.name: DateTime.Add(ISODuration) for calendar arithmetic
patterns.description: Always use DateTime.Add or ISODuration.AddTo for date arithmetic — these handle month-end clamping (Jan 31 + 1M = Feb 28) and DST. Never use time.Time.Add with fixed durations for calendar periods.

patterns.name: ISODuration.DivisibleBy for cadence validation
patterns.description: Used to validate that a billing period is an integer multiple of a rate card cadence. Tests multiple days-in-month/hours-in-day scenarios for correctness under DST.

patterns.name: Pre-defined DurationXxx vars
patterns.description: Use the package-level constants (DurationDay, DurationMonth, DurationYear, etc.) instead of constructing ISODuration from scratch for common periods.

patterns.name: DateTime.Format with RFC9557Layout for timezone-preserving output
patterns.description: Use dt.Format(RFC9557Layout) to produce timezone-annotated output. Falls back to ISO8601Layout when no named timezone is set.

key_file_guides.role: DateTime struct, Add/AddYearsNoOverflow/AddMonthsNoOverflow/AddWeeks/AddDays, shiftClockTo for wall-clock–safe arithmetic.
key_file_guides.watch_for: shiftClockTo computes wallTimeDiff then calls d.Time.Add to avoid re-triggering monotonic clock issues.

key_file_guides.role: ISODuration wrapping period.Period, DivisibleBy, Mul, Add, Subtract, AddTo (delegates to DateTime.Add).
key_file_guides.watch_for: DivisibleBy calls Simplify(true) and tests four daysInMonth × three hoursInDays combinations — a false negative means the divisor is ambiguous under calendar variation.

key_file_guides.role: Parse tries multiple layouts; RFC9557 [tz] suffix is stripped and location loaded via time.LoadLocation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Relies on strings.LastIndexByte + HasSuffix — multiple bracket pairs or brackets not at end return an error.

key_file_guides.role: DateTime.Format replaces layoutTZName placeholder with the actual Location().String().
key_file_guides.watch_for: If Location is unnamed (empty string), falls back to ISO8601 layout; do not assume RFC9557 output for UTC-offset-only times.

key_file_guides.role: ISODurationString typed string, Parse() and ParsePtrOrNil().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Parse wraps period.Parse errors in NewDurationParseError for consistent error messaging.

key_file_guides.role: MustParseDateTime, MustParseDuration, MustLoadLocation, MustParseTimeInLocation — test helpers only, live in the main package (not testutils sub-package).
key_file_guides.watch_for: testutils.go is in package datetime not package datetime_test — accessible from internal tests without import.

anti_patterns: Using time.Time.Add(30 * 24 * time.Hour) for 'one month' — use DateTime.Add(DurationMonth) to handle month-end clamping
Calling time.Parse directly for user-supplied timestamps — always go through datetime.Parse for RFC9557 support
Comparing ISODuration values with == — use ISODuration.Equal(*ISODuration) which normalises string form
Constructing period.Period directly instead of ISODuration — bypasses no-overflow arithmetic in AddTo

decisions.decision: Custom shiftClockTo for all date arithmetic instead of time.AddDate
decisions.rationale: time.AddDate can shift the wall clock in ways that break DST transitions; shiftClockTo preserves the monotonic/wall relationship by computing the diff and calling Add.

decisions.decision: DivisibleBy tests multiple calendar scenarios rather than exact arithmetic
decisions.rationale: ISO calendar divisions are ambiguous (1 year ÷ hours depends on DST); testing across [28,29,30,31] days/month and [23,24,25] hours/day provides a conservative correct answer.

code_examples.scenario: Parse, advance by ISO duration, format with timezone
code_examples.code:
import "github.com/openmeterio/openmeter/pkg/datetime"

start, err := datetime.Parse("2025-01-31T00:00:00Z")
if err != nil { return err }
end := start.Add(datetime.DurationMonth) // 2025-02-28T00:00:00Z
formatted := end.Format(datetime.RFC9557Layout)
